words.meaning_en: A written list of goods sent or work done with prices
words.example_sentence: The shopkeeper gave us an invoice for the purchase.

words.meaning_en: A place where things are bought and sold
words.example_sentence: Mother goes to the market every Saturday.

words.meaning_en: A line of people waiting for something
words.example_sentence: We stood in a queue to buy movie tickets.

words.meaning_en: A written proof that money or goods have been received
words.example_sentence: Always keep the receipt after shopping.

words.meaning_en: A place where goods are sold
words.example_sentence: She went to the sweet shop after school.

words.meaning_en: The amount of money asked for something
words.example_sentence: The price of the book was fifty rupees.

words.meaning_en: A reduction in the usual price
words.example_sentence: The shopkeeper gave a discount on Diwali.

words.meaning_en: A container made of cloth or leather used to carry things
words.example_sentence: She kept her books in her school bag.

words.meaning_en: A person who handles payments at a shop or bank
words.example_sentence: She paid the cashier at the checkout counter.

words.meaning_en: A small cart used in a supermarket to carry goods while shopping
words.example_sentence: He pushed a trolley through the supermarket.

words.meaning_en: To buy something at a lower price after negotiating
words.example_sentence: She managed to bargain for a cheaper price.

words.meaning_en: Money in the form of coins or notes
words.example_sentence: He paid for the book in cash.

words.meaning_en: A small flat case for carrying money and cards
words.example_sentence: He lost his wallet at the bus stop.

words.meaning_en: Food and household items bought from a shop
words.example_sentence: Mum went to buy grocery from the market.
